speaker: BELARIUS
text: You are not well. Remain here in the cave.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: We’ll come to you after hunting.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Brother, stay here.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Are we not brothers?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: So man and man should be,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: But clay and clay differs in dignity,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Whose dust is both alike. I am very sick.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Go you to hunting. I’ll abide with him.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: So sick I am not, yet I am not well;

speaker: IMOGEN
text: But not so citizen a wanton as

speaker: IMOGEN
text: To seem to die ere sick. So please you, leave me.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Stick to your journal course. The breach of custom

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Is breach of all. I am ill, but your being by me

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Cannot amend me. Society is no comfort

speaker: IMOGEN
text: To one not sociable. I am not very sick,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Since I can reason of it. Pray you trust me here—

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I’ll rob none but myself—and let me die,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Stealing so poorly.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I love thee—I have spoke it—

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: How much the quantity, the weight as much

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: As I do love my father.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: What? How, how?

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: If it be sin to say so, sir, I yoke me

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: In my good brother’s fault. I know not why

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: I love this youth, and I have heard you say

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Love’s reason’s without reason. The bier at door,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: And a demand who is ’t shall die, I’d say

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: “My father, not this youth.”

speaker: BELARIUS
text: O, noble strain!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: O, worthiness of nature, breed of greatness!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Cowards father cowards and base things sire base;

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Nature hath meal and bran, contempt and grace.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I’m not their father, yet who this should be

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Doth miracle itself, loved before me.—

speaker: BELARIUS
text: ’Tis the ninth hour o’ th’ morn.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Brother, farewell.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I wish you sport.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: You health.—So please you, sir.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: These are kind creatures. Gods, what lies I have heard!

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Our courtiers say all’s savage but at court;

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Experience, O, thou disprov’st report!

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Th’ imperious seas breeds monsters; for the dish

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Poor tributary rivers as sweet fish.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I am sick still, heart-sick. Pisanio,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I’ll now taste of thy drug.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I could not stir him.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: He said he was gentle but unfortunate,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Dishonestly afflicted but yet honest.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Thus did he answer me, yet said hereafter

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: I might know more.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To th’ field, to th’ field!

speaker: BELARIUS
text:
To
                 
                Fidele
                .
                 
              We’ll leave you for this time. Go in and rest.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: We’ll not be long away.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Pray, be not sick,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: For you must be our huswife.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Well or ill,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I am bound to you.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And shalt be ever.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: This youth, howe’er distressed, appears he hath had

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Good ancestors.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: How angel-like he sings!

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: But his neat cookery! He cut our roots in characters

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And sauced our broths as Juno had been sick

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And he her dieter.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Nobly he yokes

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: A smiling with a sigh, as if the sigh

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Was that it was for not being such a smile,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The smile mocking the sigh that it would fly

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: From so divine a temple to commix

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: With winds that sailors rail at.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I do note

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: That grief and patience, rooted in them both,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Mingle their spurs together.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Grow, patience,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: And let the stinking elder, grief, untwine

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: His perishing root with the increasing vine!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: It is great morning. Come, away. Who’s there?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I cannot find those runagates. That villain

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Hath mocked me. I am faint.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: “Those runagates”?

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Means he not us? I partly know him. ’Tis

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Cloten, the son o’ th’ Queen. I fear some ambush.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I saw him not these many years, and yet

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I know ’tis he. We are held as outlaws. Hence.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: He is but one. You and my brother search

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: What companies are near. Pray you, away.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Let me alone with him.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Soft, what are you

speaker: CLOTEN
text: That fly me thus? Some villain mountaineers?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I have heard of such.—What slave art thou?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: A thing

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: More slavish did I ne’er than answering

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: A slave without a knock.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Thou art a robber,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: A lawbreaker, a villain. Yield thee, thief.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: To who? To thee? What art thou? Have not I

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: An arm as big as thine? A heart as big?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Thy words, I grant, are bigger, for I wear not

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: My dagger in my mouth. Say what thou art,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Why I should yield to thee.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Thou villain base,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Know’st me not by my clothes?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: No, nor thy tailor, rascal.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Who is thy grandfather? He made those clothes,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Which, as it seems, make thee.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Thou precious varlet,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: My tailor made them not.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Hence then, and thank

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: The man that gave them thee. Thou art some fool.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I am loath to beat thee.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Thou injurious thief,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Hear but my name, and tremble.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: What’s thy name?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Cloten, thou villain.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Cloten, thou double villain, be thy name,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I cannot tremble at it. Were it Toad, or Adder, Spider,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: ’Twould move me sooner.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: To thy further fear,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Nay, to thy mere confusion, thou shalt know

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I am son to th’ Queen.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I am sorry for ’t, not seeming

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: So worthy as thy birth.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Art not afeard?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Those that I reverence, those I fear—the wise;

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: At fools I laugh, not fear them.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Die the death!

speaker: CLOTEN
text: When I have slain thee with my proper hand,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I’ll follow those that even now fled hence

speaker: CLOTEN
text: And on the gates of Lud’s Town set your heads.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Yield, rustic mountaineer!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: No company’s abroad?

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: None in the world. You did mistake him sure.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I cannot tell. Long is it since I saw him,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: But time hath nothing blurred those lines of favor

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Which then he wore. The snatches in his voice

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And burst of speaking were as his. I am absolute

speaker: BELARIUS
text: ’Twas very Cloten.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: In this place we left them.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: I wish my brother make good time with him,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: You say he is so fell.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Being scarce made up,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I mean to man, he had not apprehension

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Of roaring terrors; for defect of judgment

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Is oft the cause of fear.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: But see, thy brother.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: This Cloten was a fool, an empty purse;

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: There was no money in ’t. Not Hercules

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Could have knocked out his brains, for he had none.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Yet I not doing this, the fool had borne

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: My head as I do his.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: What hast thou done?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I am perfect what: cut off one Cloten’s head,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Son to the Queen, after his own report,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Who called me traitor mountaineer, and swore

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: With his own single hand he’d take us in,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Displace our heads where, thank the gods, they grow,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And set them on Lud’s Town.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: We are all undone.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Why, worthy father, what have we to lose

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: But that he swore to take, our lives? The law

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Protects not us. Then why should we be tender

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: To let an arrogant piece of flesh threat us,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Play judge and executioner all himself,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: For we do fear the law? What company

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Discover you abroad?

speaker: BELARIUS
text: No single soul

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Can we set eye on, but in all safe reason

speaker: BELARIUS
text: He must have some attendants. Though his humor

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Was nothing but mutation—ay, and that

speaker: BELARIUS
text: From one bad thing to worse—not frenzy,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Not absolute madness could so far have raved

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To bring him here alone. Although perhaps

speaker: BELARIUS
text: It may be heard at court that such as we

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Cave here, hunt here, are outlaws, and in time

speaker: BELARIUS
text: May make some stronger head, the which he hearing—

speaker: BELARIUS
text: As it is like him—might break out and swear

speaker: BELARIUS
text: He’d fetch us in, yet is ’t not probable

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To come alone, either he so undertaking

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Or they so suffering. Then on good ground we fear,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: If we do fear this body hath a tail

speaker: BELARIUS
text: More perilous than the head.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Let ord’nance

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Come as the gods foresay it. Howsoe’er,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: My brother hath done well.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I had no mind

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To hunt this day. The boy Fidele’s sickness

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Did make my way long forth.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: With his own sword,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Which he did wave against my throat, I have ta’en

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: His head from him. I’ll throw ’t into the creek

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Behind our rock, and let it to the sea

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And tell the fishes he’s the Queen’s son, Cloten.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: That’s all I reck.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I fear ’twill be revenged.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Would, Polydor, thou hadst not done ’t, though valor

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Becomes thee well enough.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Would I had done ’t,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: So the revenge alone pursued me. Polydor,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: I love thee brotherly, but envy much

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Thou hast robbed me of this deed. I would revenges

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: That possible strength might meet would seek us through

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: And put us to our answer.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Well, ’tis done.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: We’ll hunt no more today, nor seek for danger

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Where there’s no profit. I prithee, to our rock.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: You and Fidele play the cooks. I’ll stay

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Till hasty Polydor return, and bring him

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To dinner presently.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Poor sick Fidele.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: I’ll willingly to him. To gain his color

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: I’d let a parish of such Clotens blood,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: And praise myself for charity.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: O thou goddess,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Thou divine Nature, thou thyself thou blazon’st

speaker: BELARIUS
text: In these two princely boys! They are as gentle

speaker: BELARIUS
text: As zephyrs blowing below the violet,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Not wagging his sweet head; and yet as rough,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Their royal blood enchafed, as the rud’st wind

speaker: BELARIUS
text: That by the top doth take the mountain pine

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And make him stoop to th’ vale. ’Tis wonder

speaker: BELARIUS
text: That an invisible instinct should frame them

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To royalty unlearned, honor untaught,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Civility not seen from other, valor

speaker: BELARIUS
text: That wildly grows in them but yields a crop

speaker: BELARIUS
text: As if it had been sowed. Yet still it’s strange

speaker: BELARIUS
text: What Cloten’s being here to us portends,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Or what his death will bring us.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Where’s my brother?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I have sent Cloten’s clotpole down the stream

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: In embassy to his mother. His body’s hostage

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: For his return.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: My ingenious instrument!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Hark, Polydor, it sounds! But what occasion

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Hath Cadwal now to give it motion? Hark.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Is he at home?

speaker: BELARIUS
text: He went hence even now.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: What does he mean? Since death of my dear’st mother

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: It did not speak before. All solemn things

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Should answer solemn accidents. The matter?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Triumphs for nothing and lamenting toys

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Is jollity for apes and grief for boys.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Is Cadwal mad?

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Look, here he comes,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And brings the dire occasion in his arms

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Of what we blame him for.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The bird is dead

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: That we have made so much on. I had rather

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Have skipped from sixteen years of age to sixty,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: To have turned my leaping time into a crutch,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Than have seen this.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: O sweetest, fairest lily!

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: My brother wears thee not the one half so well

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: As when thou grew’st thyself.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: O melancholy,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Whoever yet could sound thy bottom, find

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The ooze, to show what coast thy sluggish crare

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Might eas’liest harbor in?—Thou blessèd thing,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Jove knows what man thou mightst have made; but I,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Thou died’st, a most rare boy, of melancholy.—

speaker: BELARIUS
text: How found you him?

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Stark, as you see;

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Thus smiling, as some fly had tickled slumber,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Not as Death’s dart being laughed at; his right cheek

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Reposing on a cushion.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Where?

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: O’ th’ floor,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: His arms thus leagued. I thought he slept, and put

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: My clouted brogues from off my feet, whose rudeness

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Answered my steps too loud.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Why, he but sleeps.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: If he be gone, he’ll make his grave a bed;

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: With female fairies will his tomb be haunted—

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And worms will not come to thee.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: With fairest flowers,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Whilst summer lasts and I live here, Fidele,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: I’ll sweeten thy sad grave. Thou shalt not lack

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The flower that’s like thy face, pale primrose; nor

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The azured harebell, like thy veins; no, nor

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The leaf of eglantine whom, not to slander,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Out-sweetened not thy breath. The ruddock would

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: With charitable bill—O bill, sore shaming

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Those rich-left heirs that let their fathers lie

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Without a monument—bring thee all this,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Yea, and furred moss besides, when flowers are none

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: To winter-ground thy corse.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Prithee, have done,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And do not play in wench-like words with that

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Which is so serious. Let us bury him

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: And not protract with admiration what

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Is now due debt. To th’ grave.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Say, where shall ’s lay him?

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: By good Euriphile, our mother.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Be ’t so.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: And let us, Polydor, though now our voices

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Have got the mannish crack, sing him to th’ ground

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: As once to our mother; use like note and words,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Save that “Euriphile” must be “Fidele.”

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Cadwal,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: I cannot sing. I’ll weep, and word it with thee,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: For notes of sorrow, out of tune, are worse

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Than priests and fanes that lie.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: We’ll speak it then.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Great griefs, I see, med’cine the less, for Cloten

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Is quite forgot. He was a queen’s son, boys,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And though he came our enemy, remember

speaker: BELARIUS
text: He was paid for that. Though mean and mighty,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Rotting together, have one dust, yet reverence,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: That angel of the world, doth make distinction

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Of place ’tween high and low. Our foe was princely,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And though you took his life as being our foe,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Yet bury him as a prince.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Pray you fetch him hither.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Thersites’ body is as good as Ajax’

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: When neither are alive.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: If you’ll go fetch him,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: We’ll say our song the whilst.—Brother, begin.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Nay, Cadwal, we must lay his head to th’ east;

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: My father hath a reason for ’t.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: ’Tis true.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Come on then, and remove him.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: So, begin.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Fear no more the heat o’ th’ sun,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Nor the furious winter’s rages;

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Thou thy worldly task hast done,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Home art gone and ta’en thy wages.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Golden lads and girls all must,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: As chimney-sweepers, come to dust.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Fear no more the frown o’ th’ great;

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Thou art past the tyrant’s stroke.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Care no more to clothe and eat;

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: To thee the reed is as the oak.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The scepter, learning, physic must

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: All follow this and come to dust.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Fear no more the lightning flash.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Nor th’ all-dreaded thunderstone.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Fear not slander, censure rash;

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Thou hast finished joy and moan.

speaker: BOTH
text: All lovers young, all lovers must

speaker: BOTH
text: Consign to thee and come to dust.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: No exorciser harm thee,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Nor no witchcraft charm thee.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Ghost unlaid forbear thee.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Nothing ill come near thee.

speaker: BOTH
text: Quiet consummation have,

speaker: BOTH
text: And renownèd be thy grave.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: We have done our obsequies. Come, lay him down.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Here’s a few flowers, but ’bout midnight more.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The herbs that have on them cold dew o’ th’ night

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Are strewings fitt’st for graves. Upon their faces.—

speaker: BELARIUS
text: You were as flowers, now withered. Even so

speaker: BELARIUS
text: These herblets shall, which we upon you strew.—

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Come on, away; apart upon our knees.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The ground that gave them first has them again.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Their pleasures here are past; so is their pain.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Yes, sir, to Milford Haven. Which is the way?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I thank you. By yond bush? Pray, how far thither?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Ods pittikins, can it be six mile yet?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I have gone all night. Faith, I’ll lie down and sleep.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: But soft! No bedfellow? O gods and goddesses!

speaker: IMOGEN
text: These flowers are like the pleasures of the world,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: This bloody man the care on ’t. I hope I dream,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: For so I thought I was a cave-keeper

speaker: IMOGEN
text: And cook to honest creatures. But ’tis not so.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: ’Twas but a bolt of nothing, shot at nothing,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Which the brain makes of fumes. Our very eyes

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Are sometimes like our judgments, blind. Good faith,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I tremble still with fear; but if there be

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Yet left in heaven as small a drop of pity

speaker: IMOGEN
text: As a wren’s eye, feared gods, a part of it!

speaker: IMOGEN
text: The dream’s here still. Even when I wake it is

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Without me as within me, not imagined, felt.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: A headless man? The garments of Posthumus?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I know the shape of ’s leg. This is his hand,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: His foot Mercurial, his Martial thigh,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: The brawns of Hercules; but his Jovial face—

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Murder in heaven! How? ’Tis gone. Pisanio,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: All curses madded Hecuba gave the Greeks,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: And mine to boot, be darted on thee! Thou,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Conspired with that irregulous devil Cloten,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Hath here cut off my lord. To write and read

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Be henceforth treacherous. Damned Pisanio

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Hath with his forgèd letters—damned Pisanio—

speaker: IMOGEN
text: From this most bravest vessel of the world

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Struck the maintop. O Posthumus, alas,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Where is thy head? Where’s that? Ay me, where’s that?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Pisanio might have killed thee at the heart

speaker: IMOGEN
text: And left this head on. How should this be? Pisanio?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: ’Tis he and Cloten. Malice and lucre in them

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Have laid this woe here. O, ’tis pregnant, pregnant!

speaker: IMOGEN
text: The drug he gave me, which he said was precious

speaker: IMOGEN
text: And cordial to me, have I not found it

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Murd’rous to th’ senses? That confirms it home.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: This is Pisanio’s deed, and Cloten. O,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Give color to my pale cheek with thy blood,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: That we the horrider may seem to those

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Which chance to find us. O my lord! My lord!

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: To them the legions garrisoned in Gallia,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: After your will, have crossed the sea, attending

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: You here at Milford Haven with your ships.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: They are here in readiness.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: But what from Rome?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: The Senate hath stirred up the confiners

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: And gentlemen of Italy, most willing spirits

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: That promise noble service, and they come

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Under the conduct of bold Iachimo,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Siena’s brother.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: When expect you them?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: With the next benefit o’ th’ wind.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: This forwardness

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Makes our hopes fair. Command our present numbers

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Be mustered; bid the Captains look to ’t.—Now, sir,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: What have you dreamed of late of this war’s purpose?

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Last night the very gods showed me a vision—

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: I fast and prayed for their intelligence—thus:

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: I saw Jove’s bird, the Roman eagle, winged

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: From the spongy south to this part of the west,

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: There vanished in the sunbeams, which portends—

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Unless my sins abuse my divination—

speaker: SOOTHSAYER
text: Success to th’ Roman host.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Dream often so,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And never false.—Soft, ho, what trunk is here

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Without his top? The ruin speaks that sometime

speaker: LUCIUS
text: It was a worthy building. How, a page?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Or dead or sleeping on him? But dead rather,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: For nature doth abhor to make his bed

speaker: LUCIUS
text: With the defunct or sleep upon the dead.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Let’s see the boy’s face.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: He’s alive, my lord.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: He’ll then instruct us of this body.—Young one,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Inform us of thy fortunes, for it seems

speaker: LUCIUS
text: They crave to be demanded. Who is this

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thou mak’st thy bloody pillow? Or who was he

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That, otherwise than noble nature did,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Hath altered that good picture? What’s thy interest

speaker: LUCIUS
text: In this sad wrack? How came ’t? Who is ’t?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: What art thou?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I am nothing; or if not,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Nothing to be were better. This was my master,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: A very valiant Briton, and a good,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: That here by mountaineers lies slain. Alas,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: There is no more such masters. I may wander

speaker: IMOGEN
text: From east to occident, cry out for service,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Try many, all good, serve truly, never

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Find such another master.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: ’Lack, good youth,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thou mov’st no less with thy complaining than

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thy master in bleeding. Say his name, good friend.

speaker: IMOGEN
text:
Richard du Champ.
                 
                Aside
                .
               If I do lie and do

speaker: IMOGEN
text: No harm by it, though the gods hear, I hope

speaker: IMOGEN
text: They’ll pardon it.—Say you, sir?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thy name?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Fidele, sir.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thou dost approve thyself the very same;

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thy name well fits thy faith, thy faith thy name.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Wilt take thy chance with me? I will not say

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Thou shalt be so well mastered, but be sure

speaker: LUCIUS
text: No less beloved. The Roman Emperor’s letters

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Sent by a consul to me should not sooner

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Than thine own worth prefer thee. Go with me.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I’ll follow, sir. But first, an ’t please the gods,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I’ll hide my master from the flies as deep

speaker: IMOGEN
text: As these poor pickaxes can dig; and when

speaker: IMOGEN
text: With wild-wood leaves and weeds I ha’ strewed his grave

speaker: IMOGEN
text: And on it said a century of prayers,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Such as I can, twice o’er, I’ll weep and sigh,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: And leaving so his service, follow you,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: So please you entertain me.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Ay, good youth,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And rather father thee than master thee.—My friends,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: The boy hath taught us manly duties. Let us

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Find out the prettiest daisied plot we can,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And make him with our pikes and partisans

speaker: LUCIUS
text: A grave. Come, arm him.—Boy, he’s preferred

speaker: LUCIUS
text: By thee to us, and he shall be interred

speaker: LUCIUS
text: As soldiers can. Be cheerful; wipe thine eyes.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Some falls are means the happier to arise.
